100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 1, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, discussing disarmament and the ongoing tensions in Europe following the Great War. In the United States, the Supreme Court heard arguments on the legality of the National Industrial Recovery Act, a pivotal piece of legislation aimed at revitalizing the economy. Meanwhile, in the realm of science, notable advances in radio technology were making waves, with increased broadcasts captivating listeners across the nation.
Updated: Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 06:18:55 AM CST
